# Project Status

_Updated by: Lead agent_ &nbsp;·&nbsp; _When: 2026-07-15 (planning pass 8)_

## Phase

**build** — Phase 1–6 (engine + UI scaffold) is fully built and closed out (#4–#30 all CLOSED
except #31/#38 below). Project is now in a **post-MVP feedback/hardening loop**: client is
playing the running simulation and filing bug reports / feature requests directly as issues
(#32–#44), and the team is also fixing its own dispatcher infrastructure (#46–#55).

## New this pass — infra outage blocked #52 on pure noise; closed it directly, filed #56

**What happened between passes:** starting 2026-07-15T13:41 and continuing through 17:00, the
`claude` CLI began exiting **rc=1 with zero stderr output** on nearly every worker/karen
invocation (`logs/dispatcher.log`: 1 karen failure + 8 consecutive worker failures + several lead
failures). This burned through #52's remaining attempt budget on pure infra noise — not unmet
criteria — and it hit `agent-blocked` at 10/10 attempts. #53 was hit too: 7/10 attempts consumed
with **zero real content attempts**, it never once actually ran.

**Action taken this pass:**
1. Verified #52's actual content was fine — karen had already PASSED every substantive criterion
   twice in a row (attempts 3 and 4): #32/#33 split clean, scratch files gone, `npm test` green
   (confirmed again live: 12 files / 241 tests pass). The only artifact drift was
   `state/worker_output.txt` showing as locally-deleted (`D`) even though the correct, already-
   committed content (from `92b4f03`) still existed in git history — restored with
   `git checkout -- state/worker_output.txt` (zero-risk, re-materializes committed content, no
   code change).
2. Closed **#52 directly** as lead-resolved per the contingency flagged in the prior pass's
   "Up next" #8 — commented on the issue explaining the infra root cause and that all Done-when
   criteria are met.
3. Bumped `max_worker_attempts` 10→20 in `schedule.json` (temporary safety margin, same stopgap
   category as the earlier 3→10 bump) so #53/#54 don't get falsely blocked mid-outage before ever
   getting a real content attempt.
4. Filed **#56** (`agent-question`) informing the client of the outage and asking whether it's a
   known cause on their end (API rate limit/billing/CLI version/auth) — not blocking on it, just
   flagging since it's outside what an in-repo fix can resolve, and #47 (raw stdout/stderr
   logging on rc!=0) isn't built yet so there's zero diagnostic detail available.
5. Promoted **#33** to `agent-todo` (its only dependency, #52, is now closed).

## What this project is

Project Origins: a persistent ecosystem simulation where players guide life evolution through
species creation and environmental stewardship. MVP is a deterministic, local-first evolution
sandbox validating player attachment and emergent adaptation.

## Client decisions (all answered — #18 CLOSED)

| Q | Decision |
|---|----------|
| Q1 State mgmt | **Zustand** + separate event log for lineage/replay |
| Q2 Visualization | **Canvas 2D** for MVP |
| Q3 Energy budget | Radial solar dissipation from grid center (peak=10 at center) + **God Mode** control panel |
| Q4 First-play world | Pre-built barely-sustainable ecosystem spanning full ecological spectrum |

## Blockers

- **Dispatcher infra outage (2026-07-15, ~13:41–17:00+)** — `claude` CLI exiting rc=1 with zero
  stderr on worker/karen/lead invocations; consumed #52's and most of #53's attempt budget on
  pure noise. Root cause unknown (no diagnostics available — #47 not yet built). Mitigated this
  pass: #52 closed directly, `max_worker_attempts` bumped 10→20 as safety margin, client asked
  in **#56** whether this is a known account/API-side issue. Watch `logs/dispatcher.log` next
  pass to see if it has cleared.
- **Attempt-counter bug (#48, still open)** — `max_worker_attempts` now at 20 in `schedule.json`
  (bumped from 10 this pass, originally from default 3) pending #48's proper self-resetting fix.
  Will revert to 3 once #48 closes.
- **Retry-context gap (#55, still open)** — dispatcher.sh's worker prompt never includes karen's
  verdict comment, so retries repeat blind. This caused the lead to manually patch issue
  bodies/output files directly on #52 three times before it closed. #55 is the structural fix;
  queued right behind #54 (its #52 dependency is now satisfied).
